title: An Empirical Analysis of Moral Reasoning Explanations in LLMs
description: Evaluated 13 LLMs on 600+ moral dilemmas, uncovering reward hacking and skewed post-conventional reasoning over Kohlberg development baseline.
highlights: Evaluated 13 LLMs on 600+ moral dilemmas, uncovering reward hacking and skewed post-conventional reasoning over Kohlberg development baseline.
Statistically identified "moral decoupling," in AI justifications against ethical actions for the dilemmas, exposing vulnerabilities in enterprise AI projects.

title: AMBEDKAR: Bias Elimination Framework for LLM Alignment
description: Co-developed an SLM-LLM speculative decoding framework for real-time constitutional alignment without model retraining.
highlights: Co-developed an SLM-LLM speculative decoding framework for real-time constitutional alignment without model retraining.

Validated on localized sociocultural datasets, achieving up to a 26.41% absolute reduction in demographic bias compared to baselines, proving its efficacy for safe AI deployment for use cases such as judicial summarization.
